title: Room-temperature third-order nonlinear anomalous Hall effect in ferromagnetic metal Fe3GaTe2
authors: Zheng Dai, Shuai Zhang, Jiajun Li, Xiubing Li, Congcong Li, Fengyi Guo, Heng Zhang, Ziqi Wang, Minhao Zhang, Xuefeng Wang, Huaiqiang Wang, Fengqi Song
abstract: Berry curvature, as the imaginary component of quantum geometry, plays a crucial role in condensed matter physics. The spatial distribution of Berry curvature can be characterized by its dipole and multipole moments, which can induce the nonlinear anomalous Hall effect (NLAHE). To date, the NLAHE has been demonstrated in various materials, yet reports on room-temperature NLAHE are still limited. In this work, we report the observation of the third-order NLAHE in ferromagnetic metal Fe3GaTe2. The third-order NLAHE shows hysteretic behavior with the variation of magnetic field, where the coercive field is the same as that of the anomalous Hall effect, and the third-order NLAHE remains observable up to the Curie temperature (~350 K). The scaling analysis suggests that the third-order NLAHE may be attributed to the Berry curvature quadrupole. Our work not only provides an approach to study magnetic materials through nonlinear electric transports, but also opens up possibilities for the future development of room-temperature third-order nonlinear electronic devices.
summary: This paper reports the discovery of a third-order nonlinear anomalous Hall effect in the ferromagnetic metal Fe3GaTe2 that remains observable at room temperature. By using scaling analysis, the authors show that the effect is dominated by the intrinsic Berry curvature quadrupole rather than extrinsic scattering. The high Curie temperature of the material allows this nonlinear transport to persist up to 350 K. This finding is significant for the potential development of new room-temperature nonlinear electronic devices based on topological properties.
main_problem: The study aims to observe and characterize the third-order nonlinear anomalous Hall effect (NLAHE) at room temperature and identify its physical origin, specifically investigating the role of the Berry curvature quadrupole.
main_result: The researchers successfully demonstrated a third-order NLAHE in the ferromagnetic metal Fe3GaTe2 that persists up to the Curie temperature (~350 K), with scaling analysis revealing that the effect is primarily driven by the intrinsic Berry curvature quadrupole.
method: Electrical transport measurements were performed on exfoliated Fe3GaTe2 flakes using a lock-in amplifier technique to detect third-harmonic voltages, combined with scaling analysis of longitudinal and Hall resistances.
model_or_system: The physical system is Fe3GaTe2, a layered van der Waals intrinsic ferromagnetic metal with hexagonal symmetry (space group P63/mmc) and strong perpendicular magnetic anisotropy.
key_observables: Longitudinal resistance (Rxx), anomalous Hall resistance (RAH), second-harmonic Hall resistance (R2w perp), and third-harmonic Hall resistance (R3w AH).
important_parameters: Curie temperature (Tc approx 350-380 K), temperature range (1.6 K to 360 K), and magnetic field up to 14 T.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes that the observed third-harmonic signal is not due to spurious effects like capacitive coupling, contact junction effects, or thermoelectric effects.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows the crystal structure, device configuration, and basic transport properties (Tc and hysteresis). Fig 2 demonstrates the cubic current dependence and frequency-dependent verification of the 3w AHE. Fig 3 tracks the temperature evolution of linear and nonlinear Hall resistances. Fig 4 presents the scaling laws relating Hall resistivity to longitudinal resistivity and conductivity.
paper_structure: The paper begins with device characterization and magnetic properties, moves to the experimental demonstration of the third-order NLAHE at room temperature, provides frequency and current-dependent verification, analyzes the temperature dependence, and concludes with scaling analysis to determine the intrinsic Berry curvature quadrupole origin.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
